from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into one fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Simple phrase templates for each predicate
    templates = {
        "architecturalStyle": "{s} features {o} architectural style",
        "buildingStartDate": "construction of {s} started in {o}",
        "completionDate": "{s} was completed in {o}",
        "floorCount": "{s} has {o} floors",
        "location": "{s} is located at {o}",
        "cost": "the cost of {s} was {o}",
        "floorArea": "{s} has a floor area of {o} square metres",
        "architect": "{s} was designed by {o}",
        "owner": "{s} is owned by {o}",
        "formerName": "{s} was formerly known as {o}",
        "height": "{s} stands {o} metres tall",
        "buildingType": "{s} is a {o}",
        "developer": "{s} was developed by {o}",
        "tenant": "{s} has tenant {o}",
        "isPartOf": "{s} is part of {o}",
        "country": "{s} is in {o}",
        "currentTenants": "{s} currently houses {o}",
        "address": "{s} is located at {o}",
        "inaugurationDate": "{s} was inaugurated on {o}",
        "birthPlace": "{s} was born in {o}",
        "deathPlace": "{s} died in {o}",
        "bedCount": "{s} has {o} beds",
        "region": "{s} is in the {o} region",
        "state": "{s} is in the state of {o}",
        "website": "the website of {s} is {o}",
        "yearOfConstruction": "{s} was constructed in {o}",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": "the NRHP reference number for {s} is {o}",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": "{s} was added to the NRHP on {o}",
        "significantBuilding": "{s} is known for the significant building {o}",
        "governingBody": "the governing body of {s} is {o}",
        "leader": "the leader of {s} is {o}",
        "chancellor": "the chancellor of {s} is {o}",
        "governmentType": "the government type of {s} is {o}",
        "capital": "the capital of {s} is {o}",
        "language": "the language of {s} is {o}",
        "leaderTitle": "the leader title of {s} is {o}",
        "currency": "the currency of {s} is {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": "the ethnic group in {s} includes {o}",
        "origin": "the origin of {s} is {o}",
        "significantProject": "{s} is known for the significant project {o}",
        "foundationPlace": "{s} was founded in {o}",
        "demonym": "the demonym for {s} is {o}",
        "numberOfRooms": "{s} has {o} rooms",
        "keyPerson": "the key person of {s} is {o}",
        "architecture": "the architecture of {s} is {o}",
        "postalCode": "the postal code of {s} is {o}",
    }

    def _clean(o: str) -> str:
        """Remove surrounding quotes from literal strings."""
        o = o.strip()
        if (o.startswith('"') and o.endswith('"')) or (o.startswith("'") and o.endswith("'")):
            return o[1:-1]
        return o

    # Group phrases by subject to create concise clauses
    subject_phrases = {}
    subject_order = []
    for t in triples:
        obj = _clean(t.object)
        tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate)
        if tmpl:
            phrase = tmpl.format(s=t.subject, o=obj)
        else:
            phrase = f"{t.subject} {t.predicate} {obj}"

        # If the phrase starts with the subject, drop it for later concatenation
        if phrase.startswith(t.subject):
            phrase_body = phrase[len(t.subject):].lstrip()
        else:
            phrase_body = phrase

        if t.subject not in subject_phrases:
            subject_phrases[t.subject] = []
            subject_order.append(t.subject)
        subject_phrases[t.subject].append(phrase_body)

    # Build the final sentence as one complex sentence with relative clauses
    # First, identify the main building subject (the one with most triples)
    main_subject = max(subject_order, key=lambda s: len(subject_phrases[s])) if subject_order else None

    if not main_subject:
        return ""

    # Build phrases for main subject
    main_parts = subject_phrases.get(main_subject, [])

    # Build the core sentence for the main subject
    if len(main_parts) == 0:
        return main_subject + "."
    elif len(main_parts) == 1:
        core = f"{main_subject} {main_parts[0]}" if not main_parts[0].lower().startswith(main_subject.lower()) else f"{main_subject}{main_parts[0]}"
    else:
        # Multiple attributes - join with commas and "and"
        first = main_parts[0]
        if not first.lower().startswith(("is ", "was ", "has ", "stands ", "features ", "located")):
            first = f"is {first}"
        core = f"{main_subject} {first}"
        if len(main_parts) > 2:
            middle = ", ".join(main_parts[1:-1])
            core += f", {middle}"
        core += f", and {main_parts[-1]}"

    # Find related subjects (location, country, etc.) that connect to main subject
    related_info = []
    for subj in subject_order:
        if subj == main_subject:
            continue
        # Check if this subject is connected via location/country/isPartOf predicates
        for t in triples:
            if t.subject == subj and t.predicate in ["country", "isPartOf", "region", "state", "location"]:
                obj = _clean(t.object)
                # Check if the object matches main_subject or is related
                if obj == main_subject or main_subject in obj:
                    # Add relative clause
                    if t.predicate == "country":
                        related_info.append(f"which is in {obj}")
                    elif t.predicate == "isPartOf":
                        related_info.append(f"which is part of {obj}")
                    else:
                        related_info.append(f"located in {obj}")
                    break
            elif t.object == main_subject and t.predicate in ["country", "isPartOf"]:
                # The main subject is the object of this triple
                related_info.append(f"which is in {_clean(t.object)}")
                break

    # Combine core with related information using relative clauses
    if related_info:
        sentence = core + ", " + ", ".join(related_info) + "."
    else:
        sentence = core + "."

    # Capitalise first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
